A test script that asserts false is not equal to true.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that checks the logical equivalence of the expression `false == true`. The assertion uses the `assert` function to verify that the expression evaluates to `false`, which is the expected outcome. If the expression evaluates to `true`, the assertion will fail, indicating an error in the logic. This code provides narrow functionality as a basic test to ensure that the equality comparison between `false` and `true` behaves as expected in the JavaScript environment.
